does: This paper proposes AdvDMD, a unified framework that combines distribution matching distillation (DMD) with reinforcement learning for accelerated diffusion model generation. The core contribution is using the adversarially trained discriminator from DMD2 as an integral reward model that assigns low scores to generated images and high scores to real ones. The method trains this reward model on both intermediate and final denoising states and updates it online alongside the distilled model, enabling direct supervision of entire sampling trajectories. By seamlessly integrating RL rewards into the distillation process rather than treating them as separate components, AdvDMD achieves high-quality image generation in very few sampling steps, potentially surpassing the teacher diffusion model's performance while dramatically reducing computational cost.
connects: This work builds on the growing literature of diffusion model distillation, particularly Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) and its successor DMD2, which address the fundamental trade-off between generation quality and sampling efficiency. It extends recent efforts applying reinforcement learning to few-step generation by moving beyond combinatorial integration of RL with distillation. The paper responds to limitations in existing RL-guided distillation approaches that treat reward learning and model distillation as separate optimization objectives. It leverages adversarial discriminators, a technique with roots in adversarial training and GANs, adapting them as reward models that evaluate denoising trajectories rather than just final outputs.
enables: This unified approach opens pathways for more efficient and integrated training of accelerated generative models, potentially enabling practical deployment of high-quality diffusion models on resource-constrained devices. The technique of using adversarially trained discriminators as trajectory-level reward models could extend beyond diffusion to other sequential generation tasks. Future work might explore adaptive reward schedules, application to other modalities like text or video, or theoretical analysis of why joint optimization of rewards and distillation outperforms separate pipelines. The ability to exceed teacher model quality in few-step regimes could fundamentally shift how practitioners approach the speed-quality trade-off in generative modeling.
